Logistic regression h1

Elena C

2023-11-20

H1 states: “There is an effect of scene category (i.e., a difference between images showing man-made vs. natural environments) on the amplitude of the N1 component, i.e. the first major negative EEG voltage deflection.”

The data table included in the analysis contains info on the software, high-pass cutoff values, reference, sampling rate, number of excluded subjects, baseline window length, topographic region, number of channels included in the region, multiple comparisons method, statistical method, and whether the finding was significant or not for the h1 testing.

Load packages

rm(list = ls())
library(tidyverse)
library(broom)
library(readxl)

Load data and inspect it for typos

data <- read_excel("/Users/ecesnaite/Desktop/BuschLab/EEGManyPipelines/git/EEGManyPipelines-personal/Matlab/Big Analysis/initial_variables_h1_pval.xlsx")
data <- as.data.frame(data)
#correct values
table(data$software)
## 
##          besa    brainstorm   brainvision        custom        eeglab 
##             1             5            12             3            44 
## eeglab_erplab   eeglab_limo     fieldtrip     mnepython         other 
##            13             1            29            43            13 
##             R           spm 
##             1             3
data$software[data$software== 'eeglab_erplab'] <- 'eeglab'
data$software[data$software== 'eeglab_limo'] <- 'eeglab'

data <- data[-which(data$mc_method_h1=='rft'),]#high multicollinearity with spm
#table(data$hf_cutoff)
#table(data$software)
#table(data$result_h1)
#hist(data$pval)
#hist(data$pval[data$pval<0.05])

Inspect the data for outliers

Since these are real life preprocessing practices, should we consider possible outliers?

hf_out <- boxplot(data$hf_cutoff)$out
data$hf_cutoff[which(data$hf_cutoff == hf_out)] <- mean((data$hf_cutoff), na.rm = T)

Inspect for normality

# Select only numeric predictors
data_reduced <- data[,c(-1,-13)] #delete the row numbers and binary reponse to hypothesis testing
data_reduced <- na.omit(data_reduced)
mynumericdata <- data_reduced %>%
  dplyr::select_if(is.numeric) 

shapiro.test(mynumericdata$hf_cutoff)#no data is normal
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  mynumericdata$hf_cutoff
## W = 0.75238, p-value = 1.004e-14
pval_zscore <- qnorm(data_reduced$pval)

indx_inf <- which(!is.finite(pval_zscore))
pval_zscore <- pval_zscore[-indx_inf] # remove the infinite value due to a mistakenly assigned 0 to pval
data_reduced <- data_reduced[-indx_inf,]

#normalise numeric data
for (i in 1:length(data_reduced)) {
  
  if (is.numeric(data_reduced[,i])){
    data_reduced[,i] <- data_reduced[,i]-mean(data_reduced[,i])/sd(data_reduced[,i])
  }
}
# scaled_data <- mutate_if(data, is.numeric, scale)
#table(scaled_data$pval)

data_reduced$pval <- pval_zscore
hist(data_reduced$pval)

#hist(data$hf_cutoff-mean(data$hf_cutoff)/sd(data$hf_cutoff))

Check pvalues for true/false h0 testing

# Boxplot basic
data %>%
  ggplot( aes(x=result_h1, y=pval, fill=result_h1)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme(
    legend.position="none",
    plot.title = element_text(size=11)
  ) 
## Warning: Removed 12 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_boxplot()`).

#zscored
data %>%
  ggplot( aes(x=result_h1, y=qnorm(pval), fill=result_h1)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme(
    legend.position="none",
    plot.title = element_text(size=11)
  ) 
## Warning: Removed 13 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_boxplot()`).

Build a logistic model

model <- glm(pval ~., data = data_reduced)

# Summarize the model
summary(model)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = pval ~ ., data = data_reduced)
## 
## Deviance Residuals: 
##     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max  
## -4.9187  -0.7214   0.0665   0.7590   3.6455  
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## (Intercept)                              -4.376e+00  2.227e+00  -1.965  0.05196
## softwarebrainstorm                        2.284e+00  1.880e+00   1.215  0.22697
## softwarebrainvision                       1.136e+00  1.816e+00   0.625  0.53297
## softwarecustom                            2.366e+00  1.997e+00   1.185  0.23863
## softwareeeglab                            1.243e+00  1.750e+00   0.710  0.47891
## softwarefieldtrip                         9.595e-01  1.736e+00   0.553  0.58157
## softwaremnepython                         1.350e+00  1.752e+00   0.771  0.44258
## softwareother                             1.008e+00  1.789e+00   0.563  0.57439
## softwareR                                -1.301e-01  2.449e+00  -0.053  0.95772
## hf_cutoff                                -3.892e-01  4.389e-01  -0.887  0.37715
## rerefavg mastoids                         5.747e-01  5.577e-01   1.030  0.30505
## rerefmastoid                              1.614e-01  3.945e-01   0.409  0.68326
## rereforiginal                            -1.002e+00  4.312e-01  -2.323  0.02200
## rerefother                                3.195e-01  7.620e-01   0.419  0.67586
## rerefunknown                              7.577e-01  1.422e+00   0.533  0.59513
## ds_fs                                     9.692e-05  1.115e-03   0.087  0.93086
## subj_excluded                            -3.647e-02  6.724e-02  -0.542  0.58870
## bs_window                                 9.666e-04  8.144e-04   1.187  0.23779
## topo_region_h1central                     7.214e-01  1.611e+00   0.448  0.65519
## topo_region_h1central-parietal-occipital  1.403e+00  1.819e+00   0.771  0.44232
## topo_region_h1centro-parietal            -1.180e+00  1.854e+00  -0.636  0.52578
## topo_region_h1frontal                    -4.711e-01  1.082e+00  -0.435  0.66419
## topo_region_h1frontal-occipital          -2.178e+00  1.414e+00  -1.540  0.12637
## topo_region_h1fronto-central              8.501e-01  8.639e-01   0.984  0.32723
## topo_region_h1occipital                   2.874e-01  1.025e+00   0.280  0.77964
## topo_region_h1occipito-parietal          -1.241e+00  7.713e-01  -1.609  0.11057
## topo_region_h1parietal                    6.467e-01  1.040e+00   0.622  0.53519
## topo_region_h1posterior                   1.254e+00  1.888e+00   0.664  0.50787
## topo_region_h1unknown                    -4.079e-01  8.127e-01  -0.502  0.61674
## nr_chan_h1                               -3.033e-02  1.897e-02  -1.598  0.11283
## time_window_interest_h1                   2.466e-04  4.497e-04   0.548  0.58459
## mc_method_h1bonferroni                    3.321e-02  6.879e-01   0.048  0.96158
## mc_method_h1holm-bonferroni              -1.099e+00  1.899e+00  -0.579  0.56405
## mc_method_h1none                          1.653e+00  6.302e-01   2.623  0.00995
## mc_method_h1other                         2.312e-01  1.009e+00   0.229  0.81926
## mc_method_h1permutation                   7.493e-01  7.495e-01   1.000  0.31962
## mc_method_h1unknown                       1.028e+00  5.560e-01   1.849  0.06716
## stat_method_h1one_sample_ttest           -1.348e+00  1.152e+00  -1.171  0.24412
## stat_method_h1other                       1.771e-01  7.254e-01   0.244  0.80760
## stat_method_h1pair_ttest                 -1.223e-01  6.648e-01  -0.184  0.85433
## stat_method_h1rm_anova                    1.834e-01  7.955e-01   0.230  0.81813
##                                            
## (Intercept)                              .

## rerefother

## topo_region_h1central-parietal-occipital   
## topo_region_h1centro-parietal              
## topo_region_h1frontal                      
## topo_region_h1frontal-occipital            
## topo_region_h1fronto-central               
## topo_region_h1occipital                    
## topo_region_h1occipito-parietal            
## topo_region_h1parietal                     
## topo_region_h1posterior                    
## topo_region_h1unknown                      
## nr_chan_h1                                 
## time_window_interest_h1                    
## mc_method_h1bonferroni                     
## mc_method_h1holm-bonferroni                
## mc_method_h1none                         **
## mc_method_h1other                          
## mc_method_h1permutation                    
## mc_method_h1unknown                      . 
## stat_method_h1one_sample_ttest             
## stat_method_h1other                        
## stat_method_h1pair_ttest                   
## stat_method_h1rm_anova                     
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for gaussian family taken to be 2.601573)
## 
##     Null deviance: 456.31  on 150  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 286.17  on 110  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 609.06
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 2

Multiple comparissons option ‘none’ shows significant resutls (p<0.01). However, directionality is unexpected. Inspect visually this relationship.

#install.packages('hrbrthemes')
library(hrbrthemes)
## NOTE: Either Arial Narrow or Roboto Condensed fonts are required to use these themes.
##       Please use hrbrthemes::import_roboto_condensed() to install Roboto Condensed and
##       if Arial Narrow is not on your system, please see https://bit.ly/arialnarrow
library(viridis)
## Loading required package: viridisLite
#inspect the relationship between mc method and zscore pvalues
data_reduced %>%
  ggplot( aes(x=mc_method_h1, y=pval, fill=mc_method_h1)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  scale_fill_viridis(discrete = TRUE, alpha=0.6) +
  geom_jitter(color="black", size=0.4, alpha=0.9) +
  theme(legend.position="none", text = element_text(size=20),axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1))+
  labs(y= "z-scored p-val", x = "multiple comparissons")

#inspect the relationship between not using mc and original pvalues
mc_none <- data[data$mc_method_h1=='none',]
boxplot(mc_none$pval, ylab = 'original pval', xlab = 'mc none')
